$schema_note: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the Convert and Flow contact-level custom field keys (PRD Section 6). This is the ONLY place field keys are spelled. caf_delivery.py writes by EXACT key from this file, keyed by contact_id, and reads every write back byte-for-byte; provisioning (anthology_registry.py provision-fields, orchestrated by provision-anthology-client.sh) creates-or-verifies exactly these keys and STOPS setup with an operator surface on any missing field or fieldKey mismatch, never a silent runtime create. History lives in the ledger and Drive; the contact fields carry the ACTIVE anthology's links only (disambiguated by anthology_active_id). Keying law: every write is keyed by contact_id, never email.
cover_field_semantics: For the cover deliverable the pair is not a Doc/PDF pair: doc_url carries the Convert and Flow media-storage image link (contact.anthology_cover_image_url) and pdf_url carries the Drive link (contact.anthology_cover_drive_url), per PRD Section 6.
rewrite_preservation_semantics: PRD Gap G10 / Trevor's ruling D9 (max TWO rewrites, original never lost): a chapter rewrite NO LONGER overwrites the base chapter pair. The base `chapter` pair holds the ORIGINAL chapter; the first editors' rewrite lands in the `rewrite1` pair, the second in the `rewrite2` pair. All three versions (original + rewrite1 + rewrite2) coexist so the producer can see the full history in Convert and Flow, not just the ledger. The rewrite SLOT is chosen at delivery time from the participant's rewrite_count (1 -> rewrite1, 2 -> rewrite2), never a static alias; see caf_delivery.py FieldMap.deliverable_for_rewrite(). A write to a rewrite pair touches ONLY that pair's two keys, so the base chapter and any earlier rewrite are left byte-for-byte intact.

cover_style_fields.$note: U8 (B8): the cover is delivered as a SET of four distinctly-styled portrait covers (the named styles + their prompt directives are config-pinned in scripts/cover_render.py COVER_STYLES, exactly one strictly typography-driven). S7 renders/uploads all four to media storage + Drive and stamps each style's displayable media-storage image link into its sample_url field (slot 1..4). The producer approves the SET (no down-select); the client picks ONE via the universal-review 'cover' SINGLE_OPTIONS dropdown (choice_field, offering choice_options by NAME). That pick copies the chosen style's media link + Drive link into the EXISTING cover fields named in target_cover_fields, which then flow to S8 exactly as the single cover did before. This block carries FIELD KEYS only; the style names here MUST byte-equal scripts/cover_render.py STYLE_NAMES in order (coherence self-tested).

artifact_type_aliases.$note: Bridges the ledger's ARTIFACT_TYPES vocabulary (scripts/anthology_state.py) to this file's deliverable_fields vocabulary above. The two were named independently and drifted (SESSION-LOG.md resume-checkpoint drift #2): the ledger names a chapter rewrite 'rewrite' and names the assembled book 'anthology_manuscript' (this file's pair is 'manuscript'). caf_delivery.py's FieldMap.deliverable_for_artifact_type() is the ONE translation point and is called by every S8/S9 collaborator BEFORE it names a --deliverable to caf_delivery.py; an artifact_type absent from this map is a hard refusal (AF-AE-VOCAB-DRIFT), never a guess. Keys MUST equal scripts/anthology_state.py ARTIFACT_TYPES exactly (self-tested); values MUST each be a key of deliverable_fields above (self-tested). REWRITE EXCEPTION (PRD Gap G10): 'rewrite' maps to 'chapter' here ONLY as the content/render FAMILY (a rewrite is a version of the chapter deliverable, rendered with the chapter house style and provers) -- it does NOT name the field pair the rewrite lands in. Field routing for a rewrite is DYNAMIC: deliverable_for_artifact_type('rewrite', rewrite_number=N) returns 'rewrite1' or 'rewrite2' so the original chapter is preserved. Calling it for 'rewrite' WITHOUT a rewrite_number is a hard refusal, never a silent overwrite of the base chapter.

provisioning_rule: All ten deliverable Doc/PDF field pairs (20 keys: the eight base deliverables plus the two chapter-rewrite-preservation pairs rewrite1/rewrite2 from PRD Gap G10) plus the three control fields plus the five U8 cover-style fields (four LARGE_TEXT sample-url fields + one SINGLE_OPTIONS choice field) are created-or-verified per client at setup by exact key (28 keys total). A missing field STOPS setup with an operator surface; runtime NEVER silently creates a field.
readback_rule: Every write is read back byte-for-byte in the same job; a mismatch is AF-AE-READBACK-MISMATCH (caf_delivery.py exit 5).

field_key_derivation_law.$note: Verified live at Wave 0 (W0.5.json surface 1). The Convert and Flow (LeadConnector v2) create-custom-field endpoint DERIVES the fieldKey; it does NOT accept an arbitrary fieldKey on create. Derivation: fieldKey = 'contact.' + <name>, where <name> is supplied already snake-cased. To land the EXACT PRD Section 6 key, provisioning creates each field with name = the intended key WITHOUT the leading 'contact.' prefix, then reads the server-returned fieldKey back and asserts it byte-equals the intended key. A mismatch STOPS setup (never a silent create).
field_key_derivation_law.exact_match_verify: server fieldKey MUST byte-equal the intended key; else AF-AE-FIELD-KEY-MISMATCH (anthology_registry.py provision-fields exit 5, operator STOP surface)
field_key_derivation_law.write_shape_downstream: the runtime write (caf_delivery.py) uses PUT /contacts/{contactId} with customFields:[{id:<field_id>,value:<string>}] — so the resolved field_id (not just the key) is required and is recorded below

provisioning.$note: The machine inventory the provisioner iterates. Every element carries the immutable CONTRACT (intended_key, create_name, data_type, deliverable, slot) plus RESOLVED slots that are NULL in this committed template and are filled per box, in place, by anthology_registry.py provision-fields as the node user. The committed template MUST ship with every resolved slot null: no client-specific field id, no location value, no timestamp is ever committed. On a client box the installed copy of this file carries the resolved values; the repo copy stays a pure template.
provisioning.data_type_choice: LARGE_TEXT for all twenty-seven free-text keys (PRD Gap G11 reconciliation; U10 rewrite-preservation + U8 cover-style). Live Convert and Flow provisions every free-text field as LARGE_TEXT, and Trevor's every-text-input-field-is-multi-line law requires it; the earlier TEXT declaration was a repo-vs-live drift the spec called out (the field-map lied). TEXT was byte-for-byte verified at Wave 0 (80 chars incl. unicode, spaces, pipe, ampersand, percent-encoding; sha256 sent == sha256 read-back) and remains a safe subset, but LARGE_TEXT is the live-matching, multi-line-correct type and is now the declared and created type for every free-text key here: the eight base deliverable pairs, the two U10 chapter-rewrite-preservation pairs (rewrite1/rewrite2), the three control keys, and the four U8 cover sample-url keys. The provision-fields verify path asserts fieldKey byte-equality ONLY (no dataType assertion), so create-or-verify against the existing live LARGE_TEXT fields passes unchanged; on a fresh location every field is CREATED as declared, matching live. The ONE SINGLE_OPTIONS field in this inventory is the U8 cover choice (anthology_cover_choice), whose picklist options are the four style NAMES (the client picks exactly one in the universal-review cover dropdown); the PRD Section 4 universal-review decision field is a separate SINGLE_OPTIONS that stays SINGLE_OPTIONS and is deliberately NOT in this map. Changing a live field's dataType is a provisioning decision, never a silent runtime act.
provisioning.resolution_rule: provision-fields is create-or-verify and idempotent: it lists the location's existing contact custom fields, records the field_id + server fieldKey for any of the twenty-eight already present (a no-op that still verifies exact-match), creates any that are absent (as the declared data_type; LARGE_TEXT for every free-text key, SINGLE_OPTIONS for the cover choice), and asserts every server fieldKey byte-equals its intended_key. All twenty-eight resolved -> exit 0 and this file's resolved slots are stamped in place. Any exact-match failure -> exit 5 and setup STOPS with an operator surface (AF-AE-FIELD-KEY-MISMATCH); any field the client insists must pre-exist but is absent and cannot be created -> AF-AE-FIELD-MISSING. Runtime (caf_delivery.py) NEVER creates a field: it reads a fully-resolved map or refuses.

pipeline.$note: GoHighLevel / Convert and Flow exposes NO API to create a pipeline -- pipelines are UI-only. The STANDARD Anthology pipeline must pre-exist in the client's OWN Convert and Flow account (shipped in the snapshot, or hand-built once in the UI); anthology_registry.py FINDS it BY EXACT NAME and BINDS to it using the client's OWN private-integration token (PRD Gap 9; SPEC M8; W0.5 surface 5). It probes that the token can READ pipelines/opportunities and STOPS with an operator surface (AF-AE-PIT-SCOPE) when it cannot, never a silent fallback; if the standard pipeline is absent it STOPS with AF-AE-PIPELINE-UI-CREATE, never a call to a nonexistent create endpoint. Binding to a different pre-existing pipeline via an explicit --pipeline-id is an onboarding override, never the default. The per-anthology caf_stage_map (engine stage -> resolved pipeline stage id) drives the per-gate pipeline-stage update and is NEVER hardcoded; it is resolved from this pipeline at bind time and stored in the per-box registry, not in this committed template.

resolved.$note: NULL in this committed template. Filled per box, in place, by anthology_registry.py provision-pipeline as the node user; never committed.
